definition: Approximately $2.5T in corporate debt maturing 2025-2027 with weighted average coupon of ~2.5-3.5% (ZIRP-era) refinancing at 5.5-6.5%, creating ~$50-75B incremental annual interest expense and ~$2.50-3.75 S&P 500 EPS headwind (~1.0-1.5% of earnings), concentrated in the S&P 493.
evidence: Rating agency data on maturity schedule and coupon differentials
Top-10 firms are net cash with interest expense/EBIT of ~1-5%
S&P 493 interest expense/EBIT rising from ~12% (2021) to ~18% (2025E), tracking toward 22-25% by 2027
Creates measurable quality divergence reinforcing positive correlation through earnings channel
open_questions: How much of the maturity wall will private credit ($1.5T+ AUM) absorb?
Will rate cuts reduce the refinancing cost before the wall peaks?
What is the net impact after accounting for cash-rich companies that benefit from higher rates?
